pr.title: Add the modified calcprogress that is closer on r40
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Updated the progress-calculation tool to report results closer to the r40 reference by refining which map-file sections and symbols count toward decompiled code/data size. The change adjusted data-section recognition, removed the generic "file" pseudo-section from DATA_SECTIONS, added _extab/_exidx handling, and added stateful object/symbol tracking so immediately preceding symbols can be subtracted when followed by an "entry of" line.
what_changed: Modified DATA_SECTIONS in tools/calcprogress.py: removed "file" and added "_extab" and "_exidx" alongside existing extab_/extabindex_ entries.
Added parser state variables cur_object, cur_size, and j before walking map-file symbols.

Changed handling of "entry of" lines: instead of skipping them unconditionally, the script now checks whether the prior parsed symbol was immediately before the entry line and subtracts that prior symbol's size from decompiled code or data totals.
Kept ignoring "UNUSED" lines, but split that case from "entry of" handling.
Added object-change tracking using match_obj.group("Object").strip(); when the object file changes, the current symbol is skipped for accumulation.
Stored each matched symbol size in cur_size and reused it for code/data accumulation instead of converting the Size group inline twice.
smart_moves: The fix avoids treating some map-file bookkeeping as decompiled progress by subtracting the previous symbol size when an adjacent "entry of" marker indicates it should not count.

Tracking the current object file gives the progress script more context than line-by-line symbol matching alone, which is important for map files where section/object boundaries affect whether a symbol should be counted.
Updating DATA_SECTIONS to include _extab and _exidx improves classification of exception-related sections instead of falling back to text accounting.
Removing "file" from DATA_SECTIONS suggests the tool no longer treats that pseudo-name as real data contribution, reducing inflated progress totals.
decomp_lessons: Progress tooling needs to mirror linker map semantics closely; section names, pseudo-symbols, and object-boundary markers can materially affect reported decompilation percentages.
When calculating matched bytes from map output, ignore or undo accounting for metadata lines such as "UNUSED", "entry of", file-wide section markers, *fill*, and section labels.
A small parser state machine can be more accurate than a stateless regex pass when map-file lines have meaning based on adjacency or object transitions.
Keep progress scripts aligned with the target build/reference revision; this PR specifically aimed to make calcprogress closer on r40.
naming_conventions: Progress constants use uppercase list names such as TEXT_SECTIONS and DATA_SECTIONS.
Section names in DATA_SECTIONS preserve linker/map spelling variants, including extab_, extabindex_, _extab, and _exidx.
Existing camelCase progress variables such as codeCompletionPcnt were retained; new locals used snake_case names such as cur_object, cur_size, last_object, and match_obj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use REGEX_TO_USE to extract named groups including Object, Symbol, and Size from map-file lines.
Skip symbols whose regex match fails, treating them as non-countable/assembly or non-symbol lines.
Skip file-wide section symbols: *fill*, symbols starting with a dot whose suffix is a known text/data section, and bare DATA_SECTIONS names.
Subtract the last accumulated symbol size if an immediately following "entry of" line indicates it should not contribute to decompiled size.
Classify accumulation into decomp_code_size versus decomp_data_size through section_type derived from the current section name.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that further modify tools/calcprogress.py to see whether the r40 accounting heuristic was retained or replaced.
Search for references to _extab and _exidx in map parsing or linker scripts to validate section classification across versions.
Compare calcprogress output before and after PR 300 against r40 known progress numbers to quantify the accuracy improvement.
